news.headline: Chinese Communist Party-backed publication calls for China to accelerate stablecoin development in response to rising influence of USD-pegged cryptocurrencies. Op-ed stresses importance of expanding international currency status of Chinese Yuan. Stablecoins like Tether and USD Coin gaining traction in sectors like trading and decentralized finance, reinforcing USD's dominance. China urged to strengthen stablecoin development.
news.summary: A Chinese Communist Party-backed publication has urged China to hasten its stablecoin development and enhance the global status of the Chinese Yuan in response to the growing influence of U.S. dollar-pegged cryptocurrencies. According to an op-ed in Study Times, stablecoins like Tether (USDT) and USD Coin (USDC) are playing a significant role in connecting traditional and digital currencies. These stablecoins are being widely used for trading on cryptocurrency exchanges, in decentralized finance, and in emerging markets for wealth protection, reinforcing the dominance of the dollar as an international currency. The op-ed, originally in Chinese, emphasized the need for China to strengthen its stablecoin initiatives to keep pace with this trend.

news.headline: FDA approves Telix's prostate cancer imaging agent Gozellix® (TLX007-CDx). Next-gen PSMA-PET imaging agent for prostate cancer.
news.summary: Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited (ASX: TLX, Nasdaq: TLX) announced today that the United States Food and Drug Administration has approved its New Drug Application for Gozellix® (TLX007-CDx), a kit for the preparation of gallium-68 (68Ga) gozetotide injection. This next-generation PSMA-PET imaging agent for prostate cancer has received approval, marking a significant milestone for the company. This news was shared from Melbourne, Australia and Indianapolis on March 21, 2025.

news.headline: South Korea and China to speed up free trade agreement negotiations over trade of services and investments - South Korea's Foreign Ministry.
Bitcoin NFTs, layer-2, and re-staking hype have declined. Q1 2024: $1.4B volume, now $280M. Execs see Bitcoin DeFi potential.
news.summary: The Bitlayer co-founder highlighted three overhyped Bitcoin narratives - Ordinals, layer-2s, and re-staking. Bitcoin NFT volume dropped from $1.4 billion in Q1 2024 to $280 million in Q1 2025. The layer-2 narrative saw an 80% drop as well, with many projects losing hype. However, Hu sees potential in layer-2s for Bitcoin's DeFi ecosystem. Data suggests Bitcoin DeFi is still in its early stages, with only 0.3% of Bitcoin's market cap involved. Long-term success is emphasized with Harz mentioning a 100x opportunity for Bitcoin DeFi to grow. Despite mixed opinions, some experts like Sanchez believe Bitcoin layer-2s are vital for the ecosystem's maturation, emphasizing the need for interoperability with Ethereum-based protocols for sustained growth.

news.headline: 51Talk Online Education Group saw impressive growth in 2024, with gross billings of $69.6M (+74.4%), net revenues of $50.7M (+87.0%), and active students reaching 95,000 (+87.0%). Operating loss for 2024 was $8.0M, and net loss was $7.2M. Company had total cash of $29.2M and advances from students of $45.1M by December 31, 2024. Q4 2024 net revenues were $16.2M (+117.3%) with gross margin at 77.5%. Operating loss for Q4 was $0.9M, and net loss was $1.4M. The company predicts net gross billings between $21.5M and $22.0M for Q1 2025.
news.summary: 51Talk Online Education Group reported strong financial and operational growth for the full year and fourth quarter ended December 31, 2024. Highlights include:- Gross billings for 2024 were US$69.6 million, a 74.4% increase from 2023.- Net revenue for 2024 was US$50.7 million, an 87.0% rise from US$27.1 million in 2023.- Active students with attended lessons increased by 87.0% to approximately 95,000 in 2024.- Fourth-quarter gross billings were US$21.4 million, a 93.4% increase from the same period in 2023.- Net revenue for Q4 2024 was US$16.2 million, up 117.3% from Q4 2023.- The number of active students with attended lessons in Q4 2024 increased by 83.2% to approximately 74,200.Overall, the operating loss in Q4 2024 decreased to US$0.9 million from US$4.3 million in Q4 2023. For the full year, operating loss was US$8.0 million compared to US$13.7 million in 2023, with net loss at US$7.2 million, improving from US$15.0 million in 2023. The company had US$29.2 million in cash and US$45.1 million in advances from students as of December 31, 2024. The outlook for Q1 2025 projects gross billings between US$21.5 and US$22.0 million. 51Talk emphasized the integration of AI technology to enhance operational efficiency. The company will host an earnings conference call on March 21, 2025.

Chimerix reports $140.1 million in capital, net loss of $23 million in Q4 2024, and $88.4 million for full year 2024, with pending acquisition by Jazz for $935 million anticipated to close in Q2 2025.
news.summary: Chimerix, a biopharmaceutical company, reported financial results for Q4 2024 & full-year ended Dec 31, 2024, with $140.1 million capital available, no debt, and approx. 92.2 million outstanding shares. Q4 2024 showed a net loss of $23.0 million compared to $18.2 million in Q4 2023, with increased R&D expenses of $17.7 million and G&A expenses of $7.0 million. For the full year 2024, the net loss was $88.4 million with increased R&D expenses of $74.6 million and decreased G&A expenses of $22.2 million compared to 2023. Chimerix is in the process of being acquired by Jazz Pharmaceuticals for $935 million with the deal expected to close in Q2 2025. The acquisition will not include discussion of financial results for 2024 due to the pending deal. The company is optimistic about its drug, dordaviprone, for treating glioma, and the NDA is under review with an action date of August 18, 2025.

news.headline: SANUWAVE reported record quarterly revenues of $10.3 million (47% growth from Q4 2023) and full-year revenues of $32.6 million (60% growth from 2023). Gross margin improved, with Q4 2024 at 77.9%. Q1 2025 revenue expected to grow by 45-55%. Annual 2025 revenue guidance is set at $48-50 million. CEO highlights optimism due to revenue growth and capital structure changes. Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was $3.7 million in Q4 2024 and $7.2 million for the full year 2024. Financial results were detailed for Q4 2024 and full year 2024.
news.summary: SANUWAVE Health, Inc. reported record-breaking financial results for Q4 2024 with revenues of $10.3 million, showing a 47% increase from Q4 2023. Full-year 2024 revenues reached $32.6 million, a 60% increase from 2023. The gross margin improved to 77.9% in Q4 2024 and 75.2% for the full year. Operating income was $2.5 million in Q4 and $5.4 million for full year 2024. The company predicts 45-55% revenue growth for Q1 2025 and a $48-50 million revenue outlook for full year 2025. The Company accomplished a significant reverse stock split, note, and warrant exchange during Q4 2024, simplifying its capital structure.SANUWAVE's UltraMist® system sales were strong, with 135 units sold in Q4 2024 (+70% from Q4 2023) and a total of 374 units sold in 2024 (+77% compared to 2023). Consumables revenue rose to $20.1 million in 2024, representing 61% of revenues. The Company's Adjusted EBITDA improved significantly, reaching $3.7 million in Q4 2024 and $7.2 million for full-year 2024.SANUWAVE's CEO, Morgan Frank, expressed satisfaction with the results and emphasized the positive trends in gross margin and adjusted EBITDA. The company anticipates continued growth and positive cash flows. Looking ahead, the CEO mentioned an optimistic outlook for 2025 and emphasized streamlining the operational structure. Additionally, detailed financial information and non-GAAP financial measures were provided for a comprehensive overview.

news.headline: US stocks set to open lower, but no signal of downturn, analyst says. Futures down: Nasdaq 100 -0.46%, S&P 500 -0.39%, Dow Jones -0.38%. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged in May; 10-year Treasury yield at 4.22%, two-year yield at 3.94%. Indices tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq fell in premarket. Key updates: Darden Restaurants up 6%, Accenture down 7%. Jobless claims, Philly Fed manufacturing reports, home sales, and Leading Economic Index data summarized. Nasdaq 100 in correction territory, down 11.45%.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures fell on Friday following a decline on Thursday, with all four benchmark indices down in premarket trading. Jerome Powell's comments on inflation and economic growth impacted the market sentiment. The "dot plot" still projected two rate cuts in 2025, in line with the Fed's initial forecast. The 10-year Treasury yield was at 4.22%, and the two-year yield stood at 3.94%. The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicated an 83.8% chance of unchanged interest rates in the upcoming May meeting. In premarket, the Nasdaq 100 was down by 0.46%, S&P 500 by 0.39%, Dow Jones by 0.38%, and Russell 2000 by 0.64%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) declined premarket. Darden Restaurants Inc. (NYSE:DRI) saw a 6% rise in shares, whereas Accenture Plc (NYSE:ACN) fell by 7% due to weak guidance. U.S. jobless claims slightly increased, pointing to a stable labor market in March amidst trade tensions and spending cuts. February home sales reached 4.26 million units, surpassing forecasts. The U.S. Leading Economic Index declined by 0.3% in February. The Nasdaq 100, Dow Jones, and S&P 500 remained in correction territory as of Thursday.

news.headline: Wall Street analysts predict Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL) will report earnings of 2 cents per share and revenue of $5.74 billion for Q1. The company recently closed a $1.0 billion senior unsecured notes offering. Carnival's shares rose 0.7% to close at $21.20.
news.summary: Carnival Corporation (CCL) is set to unveil its first-quarter financial results on March 21, with analysts predicting earnings of 2 cents per share, a significant improvement from the 14 cents per share loss reported the previous year. The Miami-based company is expected to post quarterly revenue of $5.74 billion, up from $5.41 billion in the prior year. Earlier in the year, Carnival finalized a $1.0 billion 5.750% senior unsecured notes offering for refinancing and reducing interest expenses. The company's stocks saw a 0.7% increase to close at $21.20 on Thursday. For more details, readers can refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga Pro.

news.headline: Saudi Arabia shares journey from water scarcity to sustainability, becoming world's largest producer of water by 2025.

This transition reflects a significant milestone in the nation's efforts to achieve water sustainability. The journey has seen the country reach a pivotal point, emphasizing the importance of water management strategies.
